_publications._publicationTitle: SVA retrotransposon insertion-associated deletion represents a novel mutational mechanism underlying large genomic copy number changes with non-recurrent breakpoints.

_contextSnippet: GenomeWalker analysis of the breakpoint regions in this patient then revealed the insertion of an SINE/VNTR/Alu (SVA) retrotransposon, which is absent from the human genome reference sequence (hg19), at the corresponding genomic position
details: Successfully revealed SVA retrotransposon insertion at deletion breakpoint in patient DA-77
